findings.summary: Processor.process() is event-driven LLM stream loop — tool execution is abstracted in AI SDK, not in opencode code
No explicit state machine — ProcessorContext uses 4 boolean flags (shouldBreak, blocked, needsCompaction, aborted) instead of enum
Two-layer loop: inner process() handles one LLM stream, outer runLoop() handles across-turn lifecycle (compaction, subtasks, MAX_STEPS)
Compaction is external fork from processor — outer loop creates compaction.create(), not handled in processor
Agent switching is entirely outside processor — outer loop re-resolves agent on each iteration from lastUser.agent field
Doom loop detection is sole agent-aware code in processor: 3 consecutive identical tool calls trigger permission.ask()
Background tasks/subagents fork entirely separate sessions — independent processing loops, parent cancellation cascades via sessionId/parentSessionId matching
Event system dual-write migration in progress (v2 events) — branching at every event handler via experimentalEventSystem flag
